page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.12xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz on the arm64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache resources include 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. This instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor, does not include local storage, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. It lacks GPU accelerators. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the use of dedicated ARM-based cores provides high compute density and efficient resource allocation for multi-threaded workloads within its hardware class.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8gb.12xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and memory throughput. Multi-core performance is highlighted by a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 24004.0 and a strong stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 81856.98. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching top-tier speeds of 435.26 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 217.09 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Passmark CPU scores falling into the average category. The instance excels in data processing tasks, showing top-tier performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression (522.44 GB/sec) and software compilation with Clang.

While the instance delivers strong performance in database operations (Passmark database score of 47924.58) and static web serving (95.89 GB/sec), certain workloads reveal performance tradeoffs. For instance, LLM text generation for small (135M) models is rated as poor at 33.18 tokens/sec, whereas text generation for medium (7B) and large (70B) models is strong. The absence of local storage means all storage requirements must be handled over the network, which is supported by the 100 Gbps baseline bandwidth. The ARM64 architecture also lacks x86-specific instruction sets like AVX or AMX. This hardware profile is suited for general-purpose workloads, database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8gb.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by 48 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz on the arm64 architecture. It features 192.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core), 36 MB of L3 cache, and a 100 Gbps baseline network interface, operating on the Nitro hypervisor without local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, software compilation, and memory bandwidth, alongside strong database operations and static web serving. However, single-core processing is average, and LLM text generation for small models is poor, though text generation for larger models remains strong. This instance is suited for database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.12xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server with 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.12xlarge is a 48-core arm64 general-purpose server with 192 GB RAM, 100 Gbps networking, and no local storage.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance featuring forty-eight dedicated cores, high memory bandwidth, and baseline hundred-gigabit networking for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.8 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory with 4.0 GB per core
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core compilation performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
